title: Component API
text: Component API CodeGloss is a Web Component with thin framework wrappers at codegloss/ framework . The configuration shape is the same in both — only the way you pass it differs. Web Component The element reads its config from a child. JSON keeps complex props arrays, objects clean — no attribute serialization headaches. Framework wrappers CodeGloss ships thin wrappers for popular frameworks. Each passes props to the same underlying Web Component: - React /docs/setup/react — codegloss/react - Nex

title: Examples
text: Examples Live demos rendered through remark-codegloss . Each example below is a standard MDX fenced code block in this page's source — the plugin transforms them into Web Component instances at build time. Click any underlined token to expand its annotation. Click a connection arc or one of its endpoint dots to see why those annotations are related. --- Memoized Fibonacci A classic memoization pattern. Annotations explain the cache lifecycle, and connections link the table declaration to its loo

title: Getting Started
text: Getting Started CodeGloss renders interactive, annotated code blocks. Highlight tokens, attach inline callouts, and draw connection arcs between related concepts. It ships as a Web Component so it works in any framework — or no framework at all. Installation That's it. One package — the web component, framework wrappers, remark plugin, themes, and config are all included. Framework-specific peer dependencies react , vue , unified are optional. Pick your usage See the Remark Plugin docs /docs/plu

title: Remark Plugin
text: Remark Plugin remark-codegloss is a unified remark plugin that transforms annotated fenced code blocks into CodeGloss instances at build time. It supports two output modes: - mdx default — emits MDX JSX. Use with MDX pipelines: @next/mdx , next-mdx-remote , Velite, Docusaurus. - html — emits a raw HTML node with the config in a child. Use with plain markdown pipelines remark-rehype → rehype-stringify or anywhere outside of MDX. How it works The plugin walks the markdown AST looking for a specifi

title: Astro / Starlight
text: Astro / Starlight CodeGloss works with Astro and Starlight https://starlight.astro.build/ using the remark plugin in HTML mode. Since Astro renders pages to static HTML, we emit raw elements and load the runtime via a client-side script. Install Configure the remark plugin Add remark-codegloss to your astro.config.mjs with output: 'html' : For plain Astro without Starlight , add the plugin under the same markdown.remarkPlugins key and load the runtime script in your layout's . Copy the runtime t

title: Docusaurus
text: Docusaurus Set up CodeGloss with Docusaurus https://docusaurus.io/ v3. Install Configure the remark plugin Add remark-codegloss to your docusaurus.config.js . Use skipImport: true because we'll provide the component via Docusaurus's MDX components map next step : Register the component Swizzle MDX components to make CodeGloss available everywhere: That's it. Every js sandbox block in your docs and blog now renders as an interactive CodeGloss instance. No CSS import needed CodeGloss bundles its s

title: Next.js
text: Next.js CodeGloss works with Next.js in two ways: 1. Direct React usage — import CodeGloss from codegloss/react and use it as a regular component 2. Authoring in MDX — install remark-codegloss and use the fenced code block syntax Install For direct React usage: For MDX authoring also installs the remark plugin : codegloss declares react as an optional peer dependency with a permissive range =16.14.0 — npm/pnpm/yarn won't error on any modern React version. Direct React usage no MDX Just import an

title: React
text: React CodeGloss ships a thin React wrapper at codegloss/react . It works with React 16.14+ and renders the same Web Component under the hood. Install codegloss declares react as an optional peer dependency with a permissive range =16.14.0 — npm/pnpm/yarn won't error on any modern React version. Usage Import and use it as a regular component. The 'use client' directive is needed in Next.js App Router because the component is interactive: Props The React wrapper accepts the same props as the Web C

title: Svelte / SvelteKit
headings: Option 1: Fenced code blocks with remark plugin
text: Svelte / SvelteKit CodeGloss ships a Svelte wrapper at codegloss/svelte . It supports Svelte 4 and Svelte 5. Install Direct component usage Import and use the wrapper in any .svelte file: No CSS import needed. The Web Component bundles its styles via Shadow DOM. SvelteKit + mdsvex mdsvex https://mdsvex.pngwn.io/ lets you write Svelte components in markdown files. CodeGloss works with mdsvex in two ways: Option 1: Fenced code blocks with remark plugin Configure remark-codegloss in svelte.config.j

title: Velite
text: Velite Set up CodeGloss with Velite https://velite.js.org/ — a content build tool that compiles markdown/MDX into type-safe data collections. This documentation site itself uses Velite + Next.js. Install Configure Velite Add remark-codegloss to your velite.config.ts . Use skipImport: true because the React wrapper is provided via the components map at render time: Render compiled MDX Velite compiles MDX content into a JS function body string. Pass CodeGloss via the components map when you render

title: VitePress
text: VitePress CodeGloss works with VitePress https://vitepress.dev/ via the Vue wrapper. Register the component globally and use it in any markdown page. Install Register the component Create a custom theme that registers the Vue wrapper globally: Configure the Vue compiler Tell Vue to treat as a custom element so it doesn't emit unknown-component warnings: Use in markdown pages Drop directly in any .md file: The :annotations prefix uses Vue's v-bind shorthand to pass the array as a JavaScript expre

title: Vue / Nuxt
text: Vue / Nuxt CodeGloss ships a thin Vue 3 wrapper at codegloss/vue . Under the hood it renders the same Web Component with a JSON config child. Install Direct component usage Import and use CodeGloss like any Vue component: No CSS import needed. The Web Component bundles its styles via Shadow DOM. VitePress VitePress uses Vue under the hood. Register the component globally in your theme: Then tell Vue's compiler to treat as a custom element prevents unknown-element warnings : Now you can use direc

title: Styles & Theming
text: Styles & Theming You don't need to import a stylesheet. The Web Component uses Shadow DOM with constructable stylesheets — all styles are bundled into the JS and applied automatically. Named themes Apply a bundled theme via the theme attribute HTML or prop React/Vue/Svelte : Bundled themes Theme Variant Based on --- --- --- github-light light GitHub code blocks github-dark dark GitHub code blocks one-light light Atom One Light one-dark dark Atom One Dark dracula dark Dracula nord-light light Nor
